tasks.description: Add the unified resize dispatcher to `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.ts`: introduce exported `ClipEdgeResizeContext` types, implement `applyClipEdgeMove(context, edge, newBoundaryTime)` by reusing `computeGroupChildBoundaryResize` and `computeGroupEdgeResize`, add pure `snapBoundaryToSiblings`, and expand `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.test.ts` to cover `free`, `interior`, and `outer` contexts plus clamp and snap cases.

Collapse `TimelineCanvas.tsx` to one clip-edge resize lifecycle: replace the three session kinds with one `ClipEdgeResizeSession`, add `resolveClipEdgeResizeContext(rows, shotGroups, positionedShotGroups, rowId, clipId, edge)` that precomputes `context` and `siblingTimes`, rewrite `handleResizePointerDown`, `updateResize`, and `endResize` onto the shared path, derive preview overrides from returned updates, preserve cursor-offset anchoring and pointer-capture behavior, and remove the temporary `[resize-diag]` log plus all `kind: 'group-child-boundary'` / `kind: 'group-edge'` session construction in lifecycle code.

Widen the resize callback surface end-to-end and collapse commit wiring: rename the commit callback to `onClipEdgeResizeEnd({ session, updates, cancelled })` through `useClipResize.ts`, `useTimelineState.types.ts`, `useTimelineState.contexts.ts`, `TimelineEditor.tsx`, and `VideoEditorProvider.tsx`; remove `startGroupEdgeResize`; refactor `useClipResize` so one commit path builds a single `applyEdit` from `updates` while reusing `computeClipResizeMetaPatch` and existing overlap/meta helpers; delete now-obsolete rediscovery helpers such as `getInteriorNeighbor`, `resolveGroupMembers`, and the outer-edge closure path.

Update resize tests to the unified contract without weakening coverage: rewrite hook tests around `onClipEdgeResizeEnd`, keep the 12 existing end-to-end resize integration tests in `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` behaviorally unchanged apart from callback plumbing, and add at least one new end-to-end free-clip resize test that exercises the unified path and asserts snap-to-sibling behavior.

Run validation and close the loop: create a short throwaway repro script for the reported resize-path ambiguity / boundary-tracking symptom, run it before and after as needed, delete it, then run `vitest run src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.test.ts`, `vitest run src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`, the broader video-editor suite, and `tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit`; if anything fails, fix the code and rerun until green. Finish with grep checks proving zero hits for `kind: 'group-child-boundary'`, `kind: 'group-edge'`, `startGroupEdgeResize`, and `[resize-diag]` in the resize lifecycle/wiring files, and confirm resize lifecycle line count decreased net.

Run tests relevant to the changed files to verify correctness and check for regressions — run the full test file/module, not just individual functions. Find and run the project's existing test suite — do NOT create new test files. If any test fails, read the error, fix the code, and re-run until all tests pass.

watch_items: Keep snap-to-sibling at the `TimelineCanvas` layer; `applyClipEdgeMove` must stay pure and only consume an already-snapped boundary time.

`resolveClipEdgeResizeContext` must compute `siblingTimes` once at pointer-down: free clips use row actions excluding the dragged clip, outer-group edges exclude all `groupClipIds`, interior uses an empty list.
Do not reimplement resize math. Reuse `computeGroupChildBoundaryResize`, `computeGroupEdgeResize`, and `computeClipResizeMetaPatch` exactly as the structural core.
Preserve cursor-offset anchoring, ref-backed preview-store behavior, pointer capture, and preview override clearing semantics; the refactor is structural, not behavioral.
The unified end path must not rediscover geometry in `useClipResize`; it should commit from `{ session, updates, cancelled }` and only branch on `session.context.kind` for metadata/origin handling.
Remove `startGroupEdgeResize` completely. Its closure-based outer-edge commit pattern is explicitly out of scope after the callback rename.
Do not touch `useClipDrag.ts`, drag behavior, save/sync/persistence, migration, projection migration, or data-shape changes beyond the resize callback wiring required by the rename.
The 12 existing resize integration tests should survive behaviorally unchanged; any rewrite should be plumbing-only, not expectation drift.

Runtime risk to watch during review: free clip resize, interior boundary drag, outer edge drag, body drag inside pinned group, cross-track grouped drag, switch to/from final video, auto-save deferral, migration load, and cursor offset tracking must remain green.
Remove the temporary `[resize-diag]` console log and finish with structural greps proving the old session kinds and `startGroupEdgeResize` are physically gone from resize lifecycle code.
Keep the synthetic group preview override internal to `TimelineCanvas` and derive it from returned clip updates rather than encoding preview concerns in math helpers.
Avoid widening scope into unrelated debt-watch areas; this change is only the resize lifecycle cleanup approved in the plan.

sense_checks.question: Does `resize-math.ts` now export `ClipEdgeResizeContext`, `applyClipEdgeMove`, and `snapBoundaryToSiblings`, and do unit tests prove all three contexts plus snap/clamp behavior without duplicating existing math?

Does `TimelineCanvas.tsx` use exactly one resize session type and one shared update/end path, with `resolveClipEdgeResizeContext` resolving context plus sibling snap inputs up front and no remaining three-way lifecycle branching?

Is the callback surface renamed to `onClipEdgeResizeEnd({ session, updates, cancelled })` across every named wiring file, with `startGroupEdgeResize`, `getInteriorNeighbor`, and `resolveGroupMembers` removed and one shared commit path in `useClipResize`?

Do the hook and integration tests cover the unified path, preserve the existing grouped/off-center resize assertions, and add at least one end-to-end free-clip resize case that asserts sibling snapping?

Did the executor run the required throwaway repro script plus the specified test/tsc commands, fix any failures until green, and finish with grep and line-count proof for the structural cleanup requirements?
Did the verification tests pass? Were any regressions found and fixed?

meta_commentary: Execute in the plan’s intended order: lock the pure math contract first, then collapse `TimelineCanvas`, then widen the callback surface and commit path in one coordinated API change, then refresh tests, then validate. The main judgment call is keeping snap logic at the canvas layer while still achieving a truly unified resize flow; do that by storing `siblingTimes` on the session from `resolveClipEdgeResizeContext` so `updateResize` remains a single path. Treat behavior preservation as a hard constraint: preserve cursor-offset anchoring, snap rules, preview-store semantics, and grouped-child/outer-edge math by composition rather than rewrites. Keep scope tight to the named resize files, and if a validation failure points into unrelated pre-existing non-video-editor issues, leave it alone and continue proving the resize slice is clean. The final reviewer should be able to confirm both structure and behavior from code plus greps: one session type, one dispatcher, one end callback, zero legacy resize-kind strings, zero `startGroupEdgeResize`, zero `[resize-diag]`, tests green, and net lifecycle line count reduced.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Trace the live resize surface across TimelineCanvas, useClipResize, timeline state types/contexts, TimelineEditor, and VideoEditorProvider
Catalog current snap inputs for free, outer, and interior resize paths
Decide and apply the callback widening to `onClipEdgeResizeEnd` and remove `startGroupEdgeResize` from the surface
Preserve resize invariants such as cursor offset, preview behavior, snap, grouped-child symmetry, and outer-edge projection
Extend `resize-math.ts` with `ClipEdgeResizeContext` and `applyClipEdgeMove`
Keep snap out of the dispatcher and at the TimelineCanvas layer
Add pure `snapBoundaryToSiblings` helper in `resize-math.ts`
Implement the dispatcher by reusing existing helpers for free, interior, and outer contexts
Expand `resize-math.test.ts` for all contexts, clamp behavior, projection, and snap helper cases
Replace the TimelineCanvas resize session union with one `ClipEdgeResizeSession` including precomputed sibling times
Add `resolveClipEdgeResizeContext` to resolve context and sibling lists up front
Rewrite `handleResizePointerDown` to construct the unified session
Rewrite `updateResize` to one path using snap helper and dispatcher output
Rewrite `endResize` to one cancel branch and one commit branch, remove diag log, and call `onClipEdgeResizeEnd`
Preserve pointer-capture and interaction-state behavior while deleting old resize-kind branches and shims
Introduce `onClipEdgeResizeEnd` through types, contexts, editor, and provider and remove old resize-end / group-edge APIs
Refactor `useClipResize` to build one commit from unified updates while reusing existing meta/overlap helpers
Delete `resolveGroupMembers`, `getInteriorNeighbor`, and `startGroupEdgeResize`
Rewrite `useClipResize.test.tsx` around the unified resize-end callback and shared outer-edge commit path
Update only test fixtures needed for callback rename while keeping grouped/off-center integration assertions intact
Add a new end-to-end free-clip resize test including snap-to-sibling assertion
Run `resize-math` unit tests first
Run `useClipResize.test.tsx` and `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` next
Run the broader video-editor suite and `tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit`
Finish with grep-based structural checks and line-count delta confirmation

validation.completeness_notes: All numbered plan steps from the approved plan are mapped. Step 1 audit work is intentionally embedded into the implementation tasks rather than split into a standalone read-only task, to keep the task list proportional while still forcing the executor to touch every named contract surface. The final task also includes the required throwaway repro script, broader suite run, grep verification, and net line-count check.
